relations: Cultural assimilation is a primary reason for language death, as dominant languages replace smaller, minority languages
Globalization contributes to language death by spreading dominant languages and marginalizing minority languages
The decline in intergenerational transmission of languages is a key factor contributing to language death
English's global spread contributes to the marginalization and eventual death of minority languages
Spanish's global spread contributes to the marginalization and eventual death of minority languages
Mandarin's global spread contributes to the marginalization and eventual death of minority languages
Language death contributes to the erosion of linguistic diversity by reducing the number of languages spoken worldwide
Language revitalization programs are established in response to the threat of language death, aiming to reverse the process by promoting and preserving endangered languages
Linguists work together with language activists and community members in language revitalization programs to document and promote endangered languages
Language activists collaborate in language revitalization programs, contributing to the efforts to preserve and promote endangered languages
Community members are integral to the success of language revitalization programs, participating in language learning and cultural preservation activities
